name: PyTorch Single GPU
description: One-command PyTorch training/inference on a single GPU with deterministic CUDA settings and checkpoint-friendly defaults.

params.script:
import json, torch

torch.manual_seed(7)
device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'
model = torch.nn.Sequential(torch.nn.Linear(128, 256), torch.nn.ReLU(), torch.nn.Linear(256, 10)).to(device)
optim = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)
loss_fn = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()

x = torch.randn(1024, 128, device=device)
y = torch.randint(0, 10, (1024,), device=device)
for _ in range(3):
    optim.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
    logits = model(x)
    loss = loss_fn(logits, y)
    loss.backward()
    optim.step()

print('DC1_RESULT_JSON:' + json.dumps({
  'template': 'pytorch-single-gpu',
  'device': device,
  'loss': round(float(loss.item()), 6),
  'samples': int(x.shape[0]),
  'status': 'completed'
}))

model_cache.behavior: Warm cache keeps recent model artifacts and reuses local weights across jobs.

params.script:
import json, torch

device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'
layer = torch.nn.Linear(64, 10).to(device)
x = torch.randn(128, 64, device=device)
y = layer(x)
print('DC1_RESULT_JSON:' + json.dumps({'template':'pytorch-single-gpu','device':device,'samples':int(x.shape[0]),'mean':round(float(y.mean().item()),6),'status':'completed'}))
